assertions.evidence: docker build -t dana-utest /home/nima/dana completed successfully and docker images reported dana-utest:latest size 233MB.
docker run started container dana-utest-main and logs printed 'Dana available at http://localhost:3000'; container remained running.

GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/ returned 200 with HTML containing
.
GET http://127.0.0.1:8080/health returned 200 application/json;charset=utf-8 with body {"status":"ok"}.
Static asset URLs /assets/index-B-LYR2qN.js, /assets/index-ABSIREl5.css, and /favicon.svg returned 200 but empty Content-Type headers instead of JS/CSS/SVG MIME types.
GET /topic/some-uuid returned 200 HTML shell while GET /api/topics returned 200 JSON array, confirming SPA fallback excludes API routes.

Created topic docker-persistence-test-mnj2yvwu via POST /api/topics, restarted container with same dana-utest-data volume, then GET /api/topics/docker-persistence-test-mnj2yvwu returned the same topic payload.
GET /api/models returned 200 application/json;charset=utf-8 with [] rather than a 500, even though internal CLIProxyAPI on 127.0.0.1:8317 was unreachable.
docker inspect .State.Health.Status reported unhealthy because HEALTHCHECK runs curl but curl is not installed in the image.
GET /api/topics returned JSON array, GET /api/settings returned JSON object with default_models, and GET /docs returned Elysia documentation HTML rather than the SPA shell.
Running container with PORT=3101 and DATA_DIR=/custom-data served /health on port 3101 and logs showed Data: /custom-data; inspect confirmed /custom-data volume mount and env vars.
docker stop dana-utest-main completed cleanly and, after restart on the same volume, previously created topic data was still readable.

Container logs show CLIProxyAPI invocation fails immediately with 'flag provided but not defined: -port', yet backend still starts; internal port 8317 refused connections, so proxy does not become healthy before backend accepts requests.

frictions: Docker runtime validation used direct docker/http commands instead of agent-browser because assertions are container/runtime focused.
Service startup is slow after restart; health endpoint became ready after 32 polling attempts in both default and custom-port runs.
blockers: Entrypoint relies on curl for readiness checks but curl is absent from the runtime image.
Entrypoint starts CLIProxyAPI with unsupported flags (-port and -data-dir), so the bundled proxy never starts.
